executionModelDetail: Direct in-session feature build, parent-expanded by bubbles.workflow because the workflow runtime lacked the runSubagent tool. SCOPE-01 implemented + unit-proven; SCOPE-02 is the live self-hosted delivery proof. The full-delivery certification ceremony was run this session and recorded in report.md + executionHistory.
certifiedReason: Certified done on 2026-06-21 by a real bubbles.validate subagent dispatch (provenanceMode subagent-dispatch). Every gate re-executed green this session with captured output + exit codes: scoped Go unit suite incl BundleSecret (SCOPED_UNIT_EXIT=0; internal/deploy ok 31.489s, so the prior spec-096 A2 failure no longer reproduces), full Go unit suite (FULL_UNIT_EXIT=0), ./smackerel.sh check (CHECK_EXIT=0), ./smackerel.sh lint (LINT_EXIT=0), artifact-lint (exit 0, PASSED), traceability-guard (exit 0, RESULT: PASSED 0 warnings, DoD fidelity 4/4), and state-transition-guard (exit 0, TRANSITION PERMITTED). SCOPE-02 live self-hosted delivery was not re-run (one-time operator-credential proof at report.md#live-calendar). The orchestrator bubbles.workflow owns the settle-commit. The spec-096 A2 fixture remains nominally routed to spec 096 but no longer reproduces a RED.

scopeProgress.name: Google Calendar write client + config + wiring

simplify.reason: The write client is one focused file (internal/cardrewards/gcal_client.go, 343 LOC) implementing the existing CalDAVClient interface; no over-engineering, no dead code, no premature abstraction.

gaps.reason: Each behavior (write+idempotent A01, disabled A02, fail-loud A03) plus the secret-key 3-mirror is unit-covered, and the live write is proven on the host. No coverage gap.

harden.reason: spec/design/scopes are coherent and hardened (extractable Gherkin scenarios + 1:1 traceability re-established this session); an additive, already-live feature needs no further hardening rounds.

stabilize.reason: Deterministic unit tests (no time/network/ordering nondeterminism); the suite ran green twice this session and the live proof ran clean. No flakiness surface.

chaos.reason: Failure paths are unit-proven (token-refresh failure, constructor validation, empty UID, config fail-loud); no new live fault-injection harness is run for this focused single-feature build and the live delivery is already proven.

docs.reason: No separate user-facing doc surface to update: the runtime config keys (calendar_id + CARD_REWARDS_GCAL_CREDENTIALS) are SST-declared in config/smackerel.yaml and the feature behavior is documented in spec.md/design.md; an internal delivery mechanism needs no end-user doc.

executionHistory.expansionReason: The active workflow runtime lacked the runSubagent tool, so bubbles.workflow parent-expanded the planning phases and authored the artifacts directly.
executionHistory.note: Grounded the gap: the card-rewards CardCalendarBridge (spec 083) is built but its CalDAVClient was never constructed (wiring passed nil + warned 'production CalDAV client is not wired yet'); the only Google Calendar usage in the tree was the READ path (internal/connector/caldav), no event-WRITE client. Authored spec.md (FR-097-01..07 + SCN-097-A01/A02/A03), design.md, and scopes.md (SCOPE-01 write client + config + wiring; SCOPE-02 live self-hosted proof).

CI build.yml cosign-signed core 73dd65dd + ml 909719f4 + self-hosted bundle; the gcal credential was sops/age-encrypted into the knb adapter (knb 1856ca3) and apply.sh --trust-model=ci-keyless redeployed core 73dd65dd healthy with calendar_sync=true and 'production Google Calendar delivery wired' in the boot log.
